Operation 'searchGoogle' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Search Google' is too generic and doesn't convey the operation's comprehensive nature. Description lacks critical input details (query parameter, filtering options, pagination) and output structure information. Enhanced version specifies the required query input, optional filtering parameters, authentication requirements, and the structured SearchResponse output format, enabling AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

data.current_description: Retrieve real-time Google Search results including organic listings, knowledge graphs, ads, local results, and various SERP features.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve real-time Google Search results with SERP features
data.description_suggestion: Executes a real-time Google Search query and returns comprehensive SERP (Search Engine Results Page) data. Accepts a search query string and optional parameters to filter results by location, language, device type, and result count. Returns a SearchResponse object containing organic results, paid ads, knowledge graphs, local results, and other SERP features. Supports pagination through the start parameter. Requires API authentication via apiKeyQuery or bearerAuth.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Search Google' is too generic and doesn't convey the operation's comprehensive nature.
